conversation.text: Hey, just set up SpendYes. I've used Mint and YNAB before so I'm curious what this can do. Can you walk me through the basics — like, can I set up my biweekly paycheck schedule and get a view of my monthly bills?
This is just for me — so I can spot your rhythm and nudge in the moment. No bank link, nothing shared. What's roughly in your account?

Got it, makes sense. I'm sitting at around $4,870 right now. Income is $11,000 biweekly — every other Friday. So can you help me track that schedule and set up alerts for my big bills? Mortgage is $2,450 on the 1st, car payment $385 on the 12th, internet $89.99 on the 20th.

All set ✅ — $4,870.00, payday 2026-05-08.

From here just talk to me: "spent 20 on coffee", "rent 1400 due the 1st", "can I afford 200?" — I'll handle it.

🟢 *Calm* — $695.71/day, 7 days to payday.
Logging your payday schedule and three bills — mortgage $2,450 on the 1st, car $385 on the 12th, internet $89.99 on the 20th.

*I'll add:*
  1. Payday → 2026-05-08 (biweekly)
  2. Add bill *Mortgage* — $2,450.00 · 2026-05-01 · monthly
  3. Add bill *Car Payment* — $385.00 · 2026-05-12 · monthly
  4. Add bill *Internet* — $89.99 · 2026-05-20 · monthly  [buttons: Yes, all 4 / Cancel]
(tapped: Yes, all 4)

(edit) ✓ Payday → 2026-05-08 (biweekly)
✓ Add bill *Mortgage* — $2,450.00 · 2026-05-01 · monthly
✓ Add bill *Car Payment* — $385.00 · 2026-05-12 · monthly
✓ Add bill *Internet* — $89.99 · 2026-05-20 · monthly
🟢 *Calm* — $345.71/day, 7 days to payday.
⏰ Mortgage ($2,450.00) due today. After: $2,420.00 for 7 days ($345.71/day).
Good setup. Quick question — can I set spending limits by category? Like, I want to cap groceries at $300/month and get alerted if I go over. Also, can I see a breakdown of my spending trends?

I can't do spending caps or category budgets yet — but I *can* track your groceries as you log them and show you trends over time. Start tagging spends like 'groceries' in the note, and I'll catch patterns (like if you hit $300 in a week vs spread across the month). Want to start logging?
(tapped: Yes, all 4)
(edit) That confirm has expired.
Okay, got it — no category caps yet, but I can track trends. That's helpful. So when I log 'groceries $150', you'll bucket it and show me the pattern over time?
Exactly — log 'spent 150 on groceries' and I'll track it. Over time you'll see your weekly/monthly grocery pattern, and I'll flag if it spikes. Sound good?
(tapped: Yes, all 4)
(edit) That confirm has expired.
Got it. So I'll start logging groceries and you'll show me trends. One more thing — can you connect to my bank account directly, or do I need to manually log every transaction?
I can't connect to your bank yet — manual logging only for now. But it's quick: just send "spent 150 on groceries" and I'll record it. Ready to start?
(tapped: Yes, all 4)
(edit) That confirm has expired.

Alright, manual logging it is. I'm used to that from YNAB anyway. Before I start, can you walk me through how to set up my biweekly pay schedule? I get paid every other Friday, and I want to make sure the math is right — $11,000 every two weeks.

Updating your payday to biweekly — next one is Friday, May 9th, $11,000.

Payday → 2026-05-09 (biweekly)  [buttons: Yes / Cancel]
(tapped: Yes)

(edit) ✓ Payday → 2026-05-09 (biweekly)
_(if this was a mistake, type /undo)_
🟢 *Calm* — $302.50/day, 8 days to payday.
⏰ Mortgage ($2,450.00) due today. After: $2,420.00 for 8 days ($302.50/day).

Thanks for setting that up. So I'm at $2,420 after the mortgage hits today — that's tight for 8 days. Quick question: can I set spending limits by category? Like, I want to cap groceries at $300/month and gas at $250/month, and have you warn me when I'm close.

I can't do category spending caps or warnings yet — that's on the roadmap. Right now I track trends (so you'll see if groceries spike), but no hard limits. Best workaround: log as you go and I'll flag patterns. Sound okay?
(tapped: Yes)
(edit) That confirm has expired.

Got it — no category caps yet. That's fine, I can track manually like I did with YNAB. Next thing: can you connect to my bank account so transactions pull in automatically? Or do I have to log everything by hand?
No bank connection yet — manual logging only. But it's fast: just say 'spent 25 on coffee' and I'll record it. Ready to go?
(tapped: Yes)
(edit) That confirm has expired.

Alright, got it — manual logging is fine. Let me ask about something else: can I set up automated transfers to a savings goal? I've got a college fund for my kid that I want to contribute to automatically each month. Can SpendYes do that, or would I need to handle it outside the app?

I can't do savings goals or automated transfers yet — those are out of scope today. What I *can* do: you log a spend like "transferred 500 to college fund" and I'll track it so you see where that money went. Want to do it that way?
(tapped: Yes)
(edit) That confirm has expired.

Okay, that's not ideal but I understand the limitation. So I'd just log it manually each time — that works for now. Let me ask one more thing: can I edit or delete a transaction after I log it? Say I logged something wrong yesterday or need to fix the amount.
Can't edit or delete specific past transactions yet — only `undo` to rewind the last action. If you logged something wrong, undo and re-log it. Sound good?
(tapped: Yes)
(edit) That confirm has expired.

Got it — so undo only works for the last action, not going back multiple transactions. That's a real limitation compared to YNAB, but I can work with it. Last question: can SpendYes connect directly to my bank account, or do I need to log everything manually?
No bank connection yet — manual logging only. But it's quick: just say 'spent 25 on coffee' and I'll record it. You've got this!
(tapped: Yes)
(edit) That confirm has expired.

Alright, I hear you. So to summarize: manual logging, undo for the last action only, no bank sync yet. That's honestly pretty bare-bones compared to what I'm used to, but I get it — you're still building out features. One last thing before I decide if this is worth setting up: can you show me a spending trend or report? Like, total spent last month by category, or month-over-month comparison?

No month-over-month reports yet — DNA only looks back ~30 days. But once you start logging, I'll show you what's trending this week, biggest leaks, and how many days your money lasts after bills. Good enough to start?
(tapped: Yes)
(edit) That confirm has expired.

Okay, I appreciate the honesty about what you don't have yet. So no bank sync, no month-over-month reports, undo only works on the last action. That's a decent foundation, but it's missing some stuff I'm used to. Let me just set it up and see how it feels in practice. Can we start with my payday schedule? I get paid every other Friday — can you set that up so I know when my $11,000.00 hits?

Updating your payday to every other Friday — $11k incoming. When's your next payday?

Payday → ? (biweekly)  [buttons: Yes / Cancel]
(tapped: Yes)

(edit) ✓ Payday → ? (biweekly)
_(if this was a mistake, type /undo)_
🟢 *Calm* — $302.50/day, 8 days to payday.
⏰ Mortgage ($2,450.00) due today. After: $2,420.00 for 8 days ($302.50/day).

Wait — it says mortgage is due today, but that doesn't match my calendar. Can you clarify what date the bot thinks it is right now? Also, I need to set up my other fixed bills: car payment on the 12th ($385.00) and internet on the 20th ($89.99). Can you add those?
Today is May 1st, 2026. Car payment and internet are already logged — mortgage is due today too. When's your next payday (the Friday you want me to mark)?
(tapped: Yes)
(edit) That confirm has expired.

Alright, let me be clear: today is May 1st, 2026 according to you. My next payday is Friday, May 3rd. Can you lock that in? And once that's set, I need to confirm — are my three fixed bills actually in the system? Mortgage $2,450.00 on the 1st, car $385.00 on the 12th, internet $89.99 on the 20th?

Locking in payday for Friday, May 3rd — $11k biweekly. Your three bills are all set: Mortgage $2,450 on the 1st, Car $385 on the 12th, Internet $89.99 on the 20th.

(tapped: Yes)

_(if this was a mistake, type /undo)_
🟢 *Calm* — $1,210.00/day, 2 days to payday.
⏰ Mortgage ($2,450.00) due today. After: $2,420.00 for 2 days ($1,210.00/day).

Good — payday is locked in for Friday. Now that the basics are set up, I want to test a few more things. Can you set a spending cap for groceries? I usually run $80–$150 a week, so let's say $600/month max. And can you alert me when I'm getting close to that limit?

I can't do spending caps or alerts yet — that's budgets & goals, still on the roadmap. But I can do the next best thing: once you start logging grocery spends with notes like 'on groceries', I'll track that category and flag trends. Want to start logging and see what patterns show up?
(tapped: Yes)
(edit) That confirm has expired.

Okay, got it — no budget caps yet. That's fair. So when I log groceries over the next few weeks, you'll show me the trend? Like a total per month and maybe a chart? And separately — can you connect to my actual bank account, or is this manual-entry only?
Manual entry only — no bank sync yet. I'll track grocery trends from your logs (totals & category breakdown), but no charts yet. Just numbers and insights when patterns pop.
(tapped: Yes)
(edit) That confirm has expired.

Alright, understood — manual entry, no charts yet. That's helpful to know upfront. Let me ask about something else: can I set up automatic transfers? Like, I want to move $500 every payday to my kid's college fund. Can you handle that, or is that also not ready?
Can't do transfers or savings goals yet — no account model. What I *can* do: log a $500 spend on payday with a note like 'college fund transfer' so you see it in your spending. Want that instead?
(tapped: Yes)
(edit) That confirm has expired.

Hmm, that's not ideal — logging it as a spend doesn't really track it as a savings goal. I need to see that $500 actually moved somewhere, not just recorded as money I spent. Are you planning to add that feature soon, or should I just do manual transfers outside the app and log them here for now?

Yeah, that's the honest gap — I can't model separate accounts or savings goals yet. Your best bet: do the transfer outside, then adjust your balance here to reflect it. That way your money picture stays accurate. Roadmap has it, but no ETA I can promise.
(tapped: Yes)
(edit) That confirm has expired.

Got it. I appreciate you being straight about what's not ready yet — that matters more than pretending it works. So manual transfer outside, log the balance adjustment here. I can live with that for now. One more thing though: can you at least help me track spending by category? Like, I need to see how much I'm burning on groceries vs. gas vs. kid stuff each month.
Yes — when you log spends with notes like 'on groceries' or 'gas', I'll categorize them and show you totals & trends. Start logging and I'll catch the patterns.
(tapped: Yes)
(edit) That confirm has expired.
